info.name: Local TTS Server
info.description:
Multi-engine, offline TTS server. All `/tts` requests return an MP3 file (`audio/mpeg`).

**Engines:**
- **Qwen3** (MLX · Apple Silicon only) — CustomVoice, VoiceDesign, Voice Cloning
- **Kokoro** (ONNX) — 54 built-in voices across 9 languages
- **Piper** (ONNX) — fastest inference, 40+ languages, ~80 MB per voice

**Base URL variable:** set `base_url` to `http://localhost:8000` (default).

**Reproducibility (Qwen3):** set `temperature: 0` and a fixed `seed` for identical output on every call. The seed actually used is echoed in the `X-Seed` response header.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Hello! This is a custom voice with emotion control.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice-8bit",
  "speaker_name": "Ryan",
  "voice_description": "Excited and upbeat",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description:
Uses a built-in speaker (Ryan) with an emotion instruction. `voice_description` sets the tone/emotion for CustomVoice models.

Available speakers: Ryan, Aiden, Ethan, Chelsie, Serena, Vivian, Uncle_Fu, Dylan, Eric, Ono_Anna, Sohee

Speed options: slow, normal, fast

Save the response body as a `.mp3` file.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "This is the faster, lighter 0.6B model with a calm tone.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-CustomVoice-8bit",
  "speaker_name": "Vivian",
  "voice_description": "Calm and professional",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description: Same as the Pro model but uses the faster 0.6B variant. Lower RAM usage (~3 GB vs ~6 GB).

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Speaking slowly and clearly for better comprehension.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice-8bit",
  "speaker_name": "Ethan",
  "speed": "slow",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description: Demonstrates the slow speed preset (0.8×). No emotion instruction — defaults to normal tone.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "This is delivered quickly, like an energetic radio presenter.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice-8bit",
  "speaker_name": "Aiden",
  "voice_description": "Energetic and fast-talking",
  "speed": "fast",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description: Demonstrates the fast speed preset (1.3×).

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Welcome to the evening news. Tonight's top stories.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-VoiceDesign-8bit",
  "voice_description": "Deep, authoritative male news anchor with a slight British accent",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description:
Generates a voice from a plain-English description. `voice_description` is required for VoiceDesign models.

Try descriptions like:
- "Warm, friendly female customer support agent"
- "Excited young child, speaking fast"
- "Elderly professor, slow and thoughtful"

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Your order has been confirmed and will arrive tomorrow.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-VoiceDesign-8bit",
  "voice_description": "Friendly, upbeat female assistant",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description: Voice design with the lighter 0.6B model.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "This text will be spoken in the cloned voice.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base-8bit",
  "sample_voice_file": "my_voice",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description:
Clones a voice from a WAV file.

**Setup:** place `my_voice.wav` in the `voices/` directory. Optionally add `my_voice.txt` containing the exact transcript — this significantly improves quality.

`sample_voice_file` accepts the filename with or without the `.wav` extension. New files are picked up immediately without restarting the server.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Cloning with the faster lite model.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base-8bit",
  "sample_voice_file": "my_voice",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "seed": 42
}

request.description: Voice cloning with the 0.6B model.

body.raw:
{
  "text": "Every call will sound slightly different with higher temperature.",
  "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice-8bit",
  "speaker_name": "Serena",
  "speed": "normal",
  "temperature": 0.7
}

request.description: No `seed` set and `temperature=0.7` — output will vary naturally on each call, like the original library default. Check the `X-Seed` response header to replay a specific result.

Default Kokoro voice — af_heart is a 50/50 blend of af_bella and af_sarah, American English female.

No `seed` or `temperature` — Kokoro uses a deterministic ONNX graph, so output is already reproducible for the same input.
American English male voice. am_fenrir is one of the higher-quality male voices.
British English female. bf_emma is the highest-quality British voice (HH hours training data).
British English male voice.
Kokoro with slow speed preset (0.8×). af_bella is one of the best American English female voices.
Kokoro with fast speed preset (1.3×).

French female voice. Language is inferred automatically from the voice name prefix (f = French).

Kokoro supports: American English (a), British English (b), Japanese (j), Mandarin (z), Spanish (e), French (f), Hindi (h), Italian (i), Brazilian Portuguese (p).
Hindi female voice. Language inferred from voice prefix (h = Hindi).

Piper English US voice. Download first:
```
python -m piper.download_voices --download-dir models/piper en_US-lessac-medium
```

The `model` field is the exact voice stem (filename without `.onnx`). No `speaker_name` needed — each Piper model IS a voice.

British English voice.
```
python -m piper.download_voices --download-dir models/piper en_GB-alba-medium
```

German voice (low quality = smallest file, fastest).
```
python -m piper.download_voices --download-dir models/piper de_DE-thorsten-low
```

Browse all 40+ supported languages: https://huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices
Piper uses `length_scale` internally for speed control: slow=1.4, normal=1.0, fast=0.75.
Piper fast speed (length_scale=0.75).
Returns `{"status": "ok"}` when the server is running.

Lists all models across all registered engines with on-disk availability.

Example response:
```json
[
  {"engine": "qwen",   "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice-8bit", "mode": "custom",  "available": true},
  {"engine": "qwen",   "model": "Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base-8bit",        "mode": "clone",   "available": false},
  {"engine": "kokoro", "model": "kokoro-v1.0",                           "mode": "speaker", "available": true},
  {"engine": "piper",  "model": "en_US-lessac-medium",                   "mode": "speaker", "available": true}
]
```

Deletes all generated MP3 files from the `outputs/server/` directory. The directory itself is preserved so the server can continue writing new files.

Example response:
```json
{
  "deleted": 3,
  "files": ["abc123.mp3", "def456.mp3", "ghi789.mp3"]
}
```

If any files could not be deleted, an `errors` array is included alongside the successfully deleted ones.
